You can use the app factory pattern with Flask.
The create_app function builds the application instance.
Flask CLI commands run inside the application context.
You cannot easily swap the create_app function per command.
However you can pass extra parameters to the CLI.
The click library allows custom options for commands.
You can define a flag on the command itself.
This flag can trigger specific logic inside the command.
The app context is created before the command runs.
You might need to modify the app after creation.
A global variable could store the command name.
This approach avoids needing environment variables.
The Flask CLI loads the app via the factory.
It does not support different factories per command.
You can check the command name in the entry point.
Then you can configure the app accordingly.
This is a common pattern in large projects.
Many developers use this method for flexibility.
